question_text: Two identical balls are dropped from the same height. Ball A lands on a hard concrete floor and bounces back up to half its original height. Ball B lands on soft mud and does not bounce at all. Which statement best explains the difference?

options.label: A
options.text: Ball A has more gravitational potential energy than Ball B

options.label: B
options.text: Ball B transfers more energy to thermal energy and sound than Ball A does

options.label: C
options.text: Ball A loses all its kinetic energy on impact

options.label: D
options.text: Ball B has stored more elastic potential energy in the mud

correct_answer: Ball B transfers more energy to thermal energy and sound than Ball A does
explanation: Both balls start with the same gravitational potential energy. When Ball A bounces, some of its kinetic energy is converted back into gravitational potential energy as it rises again. Ball B doesn't bounce, so all its kinetic energy is transferred into other forms—mainly thermal energy (heat in the mud and ball) and sound energy. Ball B loses more mechanical energy because it doesn't return to a higher position, demonstrating that energy is conserved but converted into less useful forms.
